description_long: This shotgun features a robust wooden stock with a visibly weathered texture, transitioning into a metallic receiver and a long, cylindrical barrel. The barrel is slightly flared at the muzzle, giving it a distinctive blunderbuss-like appearance. The receiver is metallic with a matte finish, and there are visible mechanical details such as a hammer and a trigger guard. The stock is full-sized and ergonomically shaped, showing wood grain and some wear, suggesting frequent handling. The trigger guard is rounded and proportionally sized, enclosing a single trigger. The overall design is utilitarian, with a mix of wood and metal components, and the shotgun appears to be a break-action type with no visible pump or lever.
description: A weathered, wood-stocked shotgun with a flared metal barrel and a rounded trigger guard, blending wood and metal in a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full wooden stock, metallic receiver, and long, flared barrel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, flared muzzle and the cylindrical shape of the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wood grain on the stock and the metallic receiver details.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, cylindrical with a flared muzzle and no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth contour and no finger grooves
